name: Ping System
description: Players place contextual markers, callouts, or alerts that communicate location and intent without requiring voice chat.
design_notes: A ping system is a communication channel with spam, visibility, and authority concerns. Designers should define ping categories, rate limits, team visibility, object tracking, expiration, mute behavior, and accessibility channels so pings remain useful instead of becoming noise.
design_purpose: Let players coordinate targets, locations, loot, danger, and objectives without voice chat.
Support faster teamwork in noisy, cross-language, or accessibility-sensitive sessions.
Provide structured communication that AI agents, tutorials, and UI systems can interpret.
player_fantasy: Players feel understood by their team even when they cannot speak or type quickly.

parameters.name: ping_request_id
parameters.description: Request identifier used for de-duplication, correction, and moderation logs.

parameters.name: ping_category_id
parameters.description: Semantic category that chooses icon, sound, subtitle, and response behavior.

parameters.name: ping_target_world_position
parameters.description: Location attached to terrain, an object, or a last-known target.

parameters.name: ping_lifetime_ms
parameters.description: Marker lifetime before automatic expiration or replacement.

parameters.name: rate_limit_policy_id
parameters.description: Spam prevention rule applied before the ping is accepted.

parameters.name: visibility_team_id
parameters.description: Team or party audience allowed to receive the marker and audio cue.

edge_cases: A ping targets an enemy, door, or loot item that despawns before teammates receive the marker.
A muted or blocked player sends pings that should be hidden for some teammates but not the whole squad.
Rapid pings are rate-limited while an urgent downed or danger ping should still get through.
The client predicts a ping at a raycast point the server rejects because line of sight or team visibility differs.
common_bugs: Pings attach to world coordinates but not moving targets, causing enemy markers to lag behind or reveal stale positions.
Rate limiting is local-only and can be bypassed by direct network requests.
Map pings and world pings use different category ids and show inconsistent icons.
Expired pings remain in minimap or subtitle queues after the world marker disappears.
balancing_notes: Low friction pings improve coordination, but unlimited pings quickly drown out important callouts.
Enemy pings may need decay, uncertainty, or line-of-sight rules to avoid becoming wallhacks.
Category wheels should stay small enough for quick selection under pressure.
accessibility_notes: Pings should include visual icons, text labels, audio cues, and optional subtitles so they are not voice-dependent.
Allow players to mute, reduce, or filter ping categories without losing critical objective or danger alerts.

implementation_notes.unity: Raycast locally for responsiveness, then send ping_request_id, category, target entity or position, and client_input_sequence to the server. The server applies rate_limit_policy_id and team visibility, then replicates accepted markers through a lightweight state channel.
implementation_notes.godot: Use client-side selection for preview but route accepted pings through an authoritative RPC. Store ping category, owner player_id, target id or position, expiry tick, and visibility_team_id so UI, minimap, and audio cues clear together.
implementation_notes.unreal: PlayerController can create local ping previews, while the server validates trace results, team visibility, and rate limits. Replicate accepted ping actors or structs to relevant clients only, and remove them by expiry tick rather than client timers alone.
implementation_notes.web: For WebSocket games, send ping requests to the server with category and target data; the server returns accepted ping ids and expiry ticks. Browser UI should reconcile rejected pings and keep world, minimap, and subtitle markers using the same ping_request_id.

example_games.title: Apex Legends
example_games.note: Uses contextual pings for communication without requiring voice chat.

example_games.title: Portal 2
example_games.note: Uses co-op pings and markers to support puzzle communication.

example_games.title: Fortnite
example_games.note: Uses map and world pings for squad communication.

example_games.title: Overwatch
example_games.note: Uses contextual pings to communicate threats, locations, and team intent.
